definition_text: Asymmetry parameter of the quadrupole coupling tensor.
Gross molecular formula is given. The same substance can appear in multiple tables when different elements are measured.
Nuclear quadrupole resonance frequency values, typically in MHz in the Landolt tables.
Experimental or computational method code reported by Landolt-Bornstein.
Nucleus at resonance, such as N-14 or Cl-35.
Quadrupole coupling constant. The Q.C.C. and eta values correspond to each other when reported in the same line.
Reference code for the table; blank entries indicate the same reference as the preceding line.
Asterisks indicate remarks in the substance footnote.
Substances are numbered sequentially within a table; index entries combine table number and substance number.
Temperature of the measurement, usually in kelvin; RT indicates room temperature.
